author: Dr. Sameer Iqbal (Interventional Radiology)
content:
IR CONSULT — UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER
Date: 4/29/2027 (MICU)

Reason for consult: Longer-term management of recurrent symptomatic hepatic hydrothorax (and ascites) in a patient who does not tolerate diuretic escalation, has needed frequent drainage, and is a liver-transplant candidate.

Relevant context assembled:
- Recurrent right hepatic hydrothorax + diuretic-refractory ascites; frequent (weekly) therapeutic drainage required.
- Hepatic encephalopathy, recurrent (on lactulose + rifaximin).
- MELD-Na in the high-20s; actively listed / in transplant evaluation.
- Just admitted to the MICU with flash pulmonary edema.

Options discussed with the team for ongoing drainage / definitive control:
- Indwelling tunneled pleural catheter (PleurX) for the hydrothorax.
- Transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS).

Plan: The choice between these (and their respective risks/benefits in THIS patient — considering her encephalopathy, transplant candidacy, infection/nutrition risks, and the goal of bridging to transplant) will be decided with hepatology, transplant, and the patient. Decision deferred pending that multidisciplinary discussion; documenting that IR is available for either approach.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Sameer Iqbal, MD (Interventional Radiology). 4/29/2027.
